PAX8-PPARG fusions are associated with ~70% probability of cancer or non-invasive follicular thyroid neoplasm with papillary-like nuclear features (NIFTP), which is considered to be a pre-malignant tumor that also needs surgical treatment. The remaining

conflict_summary:
Date=2019 Platform=
 INTERPRETATION
The performed analysis was negative for all tested genetic alterations, but revealed an inadequate proportion of differentiated thyroid epithelial cells in the tested sample. This may reflect an extensive infiltration of the lesion by non-thyroidal cells
 such as macrophages (as referenced in the accompanying cytology report), sampling of non-thyroidal lesions , or other possibilities that result in the predominance of nonthyroidal cells in the sample.

Although no mutations or gene fusions were detected, the negative test result may reflect the mutational status of non-thyroidal cells.

The specimen showed no expression of the Calcitonin and PTH genes, arguing against medullary carcinoma or parathyroid lesion. Confidence=55

conflict_summary:
Date=2019 Platform=
 INTERPRETATION
NRAS mutation was identified in this sample, but no high-risk mutations were found.  

Clonal RAS mutation indicates that the sampled nodule is a neoplasm (tumor) and not a hyperplastic nodule.

RAS mutations are found in a continuum of the follicular-patterned thyroid tumors, including benign follicular adenoma, NIFTP, and a typically low-risk follicular variant papillary carcinoma or follicular carcinoma.

Based on the results of a multi-center study of FNA samples with surgical outcome, -50% of NRAS mutation-positive nodules are histologically diagnosed as cancer of NIFTP, and the rest as benign follicular adenomas.

Surgical management (often thyroid lobectomy) is typically recommended, although active surveillance with repeat examination within 1 year may be considered in carefully selected patients.

Patient management decisions must be based on the independent medical judgement of the treating
Molecular test results should be taken into consideration in conjunction with all relevant

An isolated TERT mutation found at such level is associated with ~50-60% probability of cancer, whereas the rest of the nodules are expected to be follicular adenomas.

INTERPRETATION:
	PAX8–GLIS3 fusion was identified in this sample.
 
	PAX8–GLIS3  fusion is a common finding in thyroid hyalinizing trabecular tumor, whereas they have not been detected in a large series of papillary thyroid carcinoma (Nikiforova MN et al. Thyroid. 2019, 29: 161–173; MarchiA C et al. Mod Pathology 2019).  However a single case of papillary thyroid carcinoma positive for this fusion has been reported Yoo SK et al. PloS Genet 2016, 12 (8): ED 1006239).
 
	Although the available data are limited, the probability of papillary cancer in the nodules with PAX 8–GLIS 3 is expected to be low, as most of these tumors are essentially benign hyalinizing trabecular tumor.

INTERPRETATION
• NTRK3 fusion was identified in this sample.
• NTRK3 fusions are associated with a very high probability of papillary carcinoma. Some of these cancers may have follicular growth pattern with abundant colloid, yielding low-grade cytological appearance.
• Risk of cancer recurrence associated with NTRK3 fusion is intermediate. These tumors, particularly when present as larger size nodules, may have a higher rate of regional lymph node and possibly distant metastasis (PMID:28125451; PMID:33539547).
• In advanced cancer, NTRK may serve as a effective therapeutic target for NTRK inhibitors, larotrectinib and entrectinib, which have been approved by the FDA for treatment of NTRK-positive advanced cancers including thyroid cancer.
